title: alchemilla vulgaris var. filicaulis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. filicaulis, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its round clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers and its distinctive rosette-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of lady's mantle include its ability to soothe menstrual cramps, reduce bleeding, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for women's health. Herbal preparations of lady's mantle include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
